tldr: Ensemble of 15 ByteT5 models trained on meticulously cleaned parallel corpora, utilizing dynamic context sampling, R-Drop, PGD, and Minimum Bayes Risk inference for Old Assyrian transliteration and translation.
summary: The team built a 15-model ensemble of ByteT5 variants (base, large, XL) trained on a custom parallel corpus extracted from PDFs, train.csv, and external publications. Data processing involved PyMuPDF, GLM-OCR, and extensive regex-driven normalization pipelines to standardize Akkadian determinatives, gaps, and transliteration artifacts. Training incorporated dynamic context sampling (Zipf/Geometric mixture for length, symmetric/asymmetric allocation, and 20% random context insertion), R-Drop for regularization, and PGD for adversarial robustness. Inference used beam search (beam=4) and Minimum Bayes Risk (MBR) ensembling, which proved superior to weight averaging. Key discoveries included the benefit of large-volume noisy data, the failure of document-level training for sentence-level evaluation, and the ineffectiveness of explicit context blocks during training.
extensive_summary:
## Data Extraction & Processing
The pipeline extracted text from PDFs using PyMuPDF and GLM-OCR, categorizing files into image, text, and broken_text types. Text-based PDFs were processed via LLM APIs with Regex heading detection, while image PDFs underwent OCR, Regex heading matching, and a second-pass LLM correction. Turkish PDFs were translated via LLM + dictionaries. For `train.csv`, the team scraped the OARE website for word definitions and performed similarity matching against high-quality PDF sources to retrieve ground truth forms. `Published_texts.csv` received pseudo-labels via LLM prompts constrained by JSON formatting and specific rules (e.g., mana 60 conversion). `Publications.csv` provided fixed-page extractions.

Preprocessing applied Unicode NFKC normalization, zero-width character removal, dash/hyphen unification, diacritic simplification, and fraction/decimal conversion to Unicode fractions. The transliteration side standardized determinatives to `{}` syntax, converted subscripts to digits, replaced `×` with `.`, cleaned `x` markers, removed annotations/line numbers/pipes, unified gap symbols to `<gap>`, and applied dictionary-level hard replacements. The translation side aggressively removed parentheses/asterisks/quotes, deleted academic terms, resolved slash alternatives with capitalization inheritance, converted Roman numeral months to Arabic, expanded measurement units, fixed spacing, and mapped gaps to `<gap>`. A custom regex-based cleaning function finalized the pipeline.

## Training Strategy
Models were trained with Dynamic Context Sampling to handle misalignments and unknown test lengths. Context length K was sampled via a Zipf/Geometric mixture distribution (`k_max=10`, `geom_p=0.35`, `long_tail_mix` applied). Left/right allocation used symmetric binomial sampling. A 20% probability (`random_ctx_prob=0.20`) inserted random contextual sentences to force global topic learning. Regularization employed R-Drop (KL divergence between two dropout passes, `RDROP_ALPHA=0.8`) and PGD on embeddings (`PGD_STEPS=3`, `PGD_EPS=0.5`, `PGD_ALPHA=0.2`, targeting `shared`, `embed_tokens`, `embeddings`). Engineering optimizations included DeepSpeed ZeRO-2, gradient checkpointing, and a custom trainer. EMA was used for stability.

## Experiments & Inference
A progression of ByteT5 models (base, large, XL) was trained on varying data subsets. Adding Turkish-to-English data and high-quality publications consistently improved scores. Pre-training on publications/pseudo-labels for 5 epochs before formal training yielded the best results. The final submission comprised 15 models grouped across two T4 GPUs. Inference used beam search (`beam=4`) without sampling, combined via Minimum Bayes Risk (MBR). MBR significantly outperformed weight fusion/averaging, which degraded scores.

## Critical Findings & Lessons
Explicit CONTEXT BEFORE/AFTER blocks during training/inference failed to improve performance. Name/dictionary augmentation provided no single-model gain but was retained for the ensemble. Document-level training with very long `maxlen` was ineffective due to sentence-level evaluation. LLMs (Qwen, LLaMA) performed averagely without specialized tricks. Bidirectional training (akk->eng) showed minimal single-model progress. Crucially, initially discarded noisy data proved highly beneficial due to volume, and sampling within MBR consistently lowered scores compared to deterministic beam search.
cv_strategy: 5-fold cross-validation based on the best-performing model on the Public Leaderboard.
preprocessing: Unicode NFKC normalization, zero-width character removal, dash/hyphen unification, diacritic simplification, editorial symbol/footnote removal, fraction/decimal conversion to Unicode fractions.

Transliteration side: subscript-to-digit conversion, determinative unification to `{}` syntax, `×` to `.`, `x` marker cleanup, annotation/line number/pipe removal, gap symbol unification to `<gap>`, dictionary-level hard replacement.

Translation side: aggressive parenthesis/asterisk/quote removal, academic term deletion, slash alternative resolution, capitalization inheritance, Roman numeral month conversion, measurement unit expansion, spacing fixes, gap mapping to `<gap>`.
Manual regex cleaning and custom normalization functions.
augmentations: Dynamic context sampling (Zipf/Geometric mixture for context length K, symmetric/asymmetric left/right allocation, 20% random contextual sentence insertion).
R-Drop (KL divergence between two dropout passes).
PGD (projected gradient descent on embeddings with 3 steps, epsilon 0.5, alpha 0.2).
Name augmentation (random capitalization, dictionary norm-to-lexeme mapping).
ensemble: 15 models grouped and deployed across two T4 GPUs, combined using Minimum Bayes Risk (MBR) inference with beam=4, which outperformed weight fusion/averaging.
post_processing: Beam search decoding (beam=4) without sampling; MBR selection over generated hypotheses.
what_worked: Including high-quality publications data and pre-training on publications/pseudo-labels boosted performance.
Dynamic context sampling (Zipf/Geometric mixture) improved length adaptability.
R-Drop and PGD effectively mitigated overfitting and improved robustness.
what_did_not_work: Explicit CONTEXT BEFORE/AFTER blocks during training/inference.
Data augmentation on names/dictionaries in source text (no single-model improvement).
Very long maxlen and document-level training (test is sentence-level).
Using LLMs (Qwen, LLaMA) without special tricks (average performance).
critical_findings: Extra data initially deemed too noisy actually performed better due to large volume.
Sampling within MBR process decreases score; beam search is strictly better.
Test set is evaluated at the sentence level, making document-level training ineffective.
LLMs (14B) performed average without special tricks, contrasting with top participants who succeeded with them.
